name: OmniKit Messaging
description: Comprehensive messaging and marketing automation system with template management, contact segmentation, campaign orchestration, and multi-channel delivery

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: Api Key
credential_fields.description: Your OmniKit API key (starts with 'okt_'). The project is determined automatically from the key.

tools.name: create_template
tools.description: Create a reusable email or SMS template. IMPORTANT: Set category='campaign' for marketing campaigns, 'transactional' for receipts/notifications, 'automation' for welcome series. Returns a template ID (UUID) - save this ID to use in create-campaign. Unsubscribe link is added automatically - do NOT include it in your content. Does NOT send anything.

content.description: Message content object with channel-specific fields (e.g., {html, text} for email, {body} for SMS)

category.description: IMPORTANT - choose correctly: 'campaign' for marketing emails/broadcasts sent via create-campaign, 'transactional' for individual receipts/notifications, 'automation' for triggered sequences. For campaigns, you MUST set this to 'campaign'.

variables.description: List of template variables (optional, will be auto-extracted)

created_by.description: User ID who created the template (optional)

tools.name: list_templates
tools.description: List all saved email/SMS templates for a project. Use this to find a template_id before creating a campaign.

category.description: Filter by category: transactional, campaign, or automation (optional)

search.description: Search in template name and content (optional)

tools.name: update_template
tools.description: Update an existing email or SMS template. Modify name, subject, content, category, tags, or active status. Variables in content are auto-extracted. Unsubscribe link is added automatically - do NOT include it in your content.

content.description: New message content object with channel-specific fields (e.g., {html, text} for email, {body} for SMS)

category.description: Template category: transactional (individual messages), campaign (broadcasts), automation (flow-triggered)

tools.name: get_template
tools.description: Get a message template by ID or name

tools.name: create_contact
tools.description: Add a new contact to the messaging system. Requires at least one of: email, phone, pushToken, slackUserId, or user_id. If contact already exists, returns existing_contact_id in the data field - use that ID to send messages.

user_id.description: User ID (one of: user_id, email, phone, pushToken, slackUserId required)

created_by.description: User ID who created the contact (optional)

tools.name: get_contact
tools.description: Get a specific messaging contact by ID

tools.name: list_contacts
tools.description: List contacts with filtering and pagination

search.description: Search in email, phone, and attributes

tools.name: update_contact
tools.description: Update an existing contact

tools.name: create_campaign
tools.description: Create an email/SMS campaign. WORKFLOW: 1) Call create-template with category='campaign', save returned ID (UUID like '8e3b305e-...'), 2) Call create-segment, save returned ID, 3) Call this with those UUIDs. IDs are UUIDs from API responses, NOT names you create. Creates draft - use launch-campaign to send.

template_id.description: UUID from create-template response (e.g., '8e3b305e-38d1-44c6-a545-87c28b43bc24'). NOT a name - must be actual ID. Template must have category='campaign'.

segment_id.description: UUID from create-segment response. NOT a name - must be actual ID from the API response.

tools.name: list_campaigns
tools.description: List CAMPAIGNS (actual send instances). Campaigns use templates and target segments. This returns campaigns, NOT templates.

tools.name: get_campaign
tools.description: Get details of a specific CAMPAIGN by ID. Returns campaign configuration, stats, and metadata.

tools.name: update_campaign
tools.description: Update a CAMPAIGN's details (name, description, channel, template, segment, sender, schedule, tracking, send settings). Can only update campaigns that haven't been sent yet.

sender_id.description: Sender ID (verified email/phone to send from)

tools.name: delete_campaign
tools.description: Delete/cancel a CAMPAIGN. Can only delete campaigns that haven't been sent yet. Use with caution.

tools.name: send_message
tools.description: Send a standalone one-off message directly to a contact or email/phone. Provide either contactId OR 'to' (email/phone) - if 'to' is used without contactId, will auto-find or create a contact. This is for promotional/transactional messages, NOT for replying to customer support conversations. For conversation replies, use omnikit-messaging-conversation-reply instead.

contactId.description: Contact ID to send to (required if 'to' not provided)

to.description: Direct email address or phone number to send to. If no contactId provided, will auto-find or create a contact. Example: 'user@example.com' for email, '+1234567890' for SMS.

contactName.description: Optional name for auto-created contact (e.g., 'John Doe'). Only used when 'to' creates a new contact.

templateId.description: Template ID to use (optional, or provide content)

content.description: Message content (required if no templateId). For EMAIL: use {"html": "<html>...</html>", "text": "plain text fallback"}. For PUSH: use {"title": "...", "body": "...", "action_url": "..."}. For SMS/OTHER: use {"body": "..."}. HTML is fully supported for email channel.

html.description: HTML content for email (supports full HTML with inline styles)

created_by.description: User ID who sent the message (optional)

tools.name: create_segment
tools.description: Create a new contact segment with filtering rules

rules.description: Filtering rules for segment membership. Structure: {operator: 'AND'|'OR', conditions: [{field, operator, value}]}. Operators: equals, not_equals, contains, starts_with, greater_than, less_than, in, not_in, exists, not_exists

type.description: Segment type: 'dynamic' auto-updates based on rules, 'static' is a fixed list

tools.name: list_segments
tools.description: List all contact segments with filtering and pagination

search.description: Search in segment name and description

tools.name: update_segment
tools.description: Update an existing contact segment's name, description, or filters

tools.name: delete_segment
tools.description: Delete a contact segment (cannot be undone)

tools.name: refresh_segment
tools.description: Manually refresh segment membership based on current filter rules

tools.name: test_campaign
tools.description: Send a test email for a campaign to verify content and rendering before sending to the full audience

test_email.description: Email address to send test to (required)

test_name.description: Name of test recipient for personalization

tools.name: launch_campaign
tools.description: Execute and send a campaign to its target audience. Campaign must be created first with create-campaign.

launched_by.description: User ID who is launching the campaign (optional)

tools.name: list_conversations
tools.description: List conversations in a project with filtering, searching, and pagination

status.description: Filter by conversation status. Values: active (open/ongoing - needs our response), pending (waiting on customer response), archived (hidden but not resolved), closed (resolved/completed), spam (marked as spam)

assigned_to.description: Filter conversations assigned to a user (optional)

contact_id.description: Filter conversations containing a specific contact (optional)

search.description: Search in conversation subject and metadata (optional)

created_after.description: Filter conversations created after this date (ISO 8601) (optional)

created_before.description: Filter conversations created before this date (ISO 8601) (optional)

tools.name: conversation_reply
tools.description: Send a reply message within an existing customer support conversation thread. Requires conversation_id. Use this when responding to customer inquiries. For standalone messages to contacts, use omnikit-messaging-send-message instead.

content.description: Reply content text (optional if template_id provided)

template_id.description: Template ID to use for the reply (optional if content provided)

send_message.description: Whether to actually send the message or just create it (optional)

contact_id.description: Contact ID (required if getting from conversation)

reply_to_message_id.description: ID of the message being replied to (optional)

from_email.description: Verified email address to send from (optional)

sender_id.description: Sender user ID to get verified email (optional)

created_by.description: User ID who created the reply (optional)

tools.name: list_messages
tools.description: List messages in a conversation. Retrieve all messages from a conversation thread including content, sender, direction (inbound/outbound), timestamps, and delivery status. Use this to get conversation history, read messages, or view what was said.

conversation_id.description: Conversation ID to load messages from (optional - filter by conversation)

sort.description: Sort field and direction (e.g., 'created_at:desc' for latest first, 'created_at:asc' for oldest first) (optional)

tools.name: get_conversation
tools.description: Get conversation metadata and context (status, priority, participants, tags, assigned_to). Returns conversation info only - NOT the message history. Use omnikit-messaging-list-messages to load actual messages.

tools.name: update_conversation
tools.description: Update conversation status, priority, assignment, and metadata. Use this to change status from open to closed, modify priority, or reassign conversations

status.description: Update conversation status. Values: active (open/ongoing - needs our response), pending (waiting on customer response - use after replying), archived (hidden but not resolved), closed (resolved/completed), spam (mark as spam)

assigned_to.description: User ID to assign conversation to, or null to unassign (optional)

tools.name: delete_contact
tools.description: Soft-delete a messaging contact by ID. The contact will be marked as deleted but data is retained for audit purposes.

tools.name: contact_history
tools.description: Get the full message history and engagement statistics for a contact. Returns contact info, message history with delivery/open/click tracking, and an engagement summary with rates. Use this to check if a contact has already been emailed before sending again.

include_engagement.description: Include engagement summary with open/click/bounce rates (default: true)

tools.name: delete_template
tools.description: Soft-delete a message template by ID. Template will be marked as deleted but retained for audit. Cannot delete templates actively used by running campaigns.

tools.name: template_stats
tools.description: Get engagement statistics for templates - open rates, click rates, bounce rates, etc. Can filter by specific template, channel, or date range. Useful for evaluating template performance before reusing in campaigns.

template_id.description: Specific template ID to get stats for (optional, returns all if not provided)

date_from.description: Start date filter in ISO 8601 format (optional)

date_to.description: End date filter in ISO 8601 format (optional)

tools.name: create_conversation
tools.description: Create a new customer support conversation/ticket. Optionally include an initial message, participant IDs, priority, tags, and assignment. Returns the conversation ID for follow-up replies.

participant_ids.description: Contact IDs to include in the conversation

assigned_to.description: Agent/user ID to assign the conversation to

tools.name: close_conversation
tools.description: Close a conversation with resolution details. Optionally send a final message, set resolution status (resolved, not_resolved, escalated, duplicate, spam, abandoned), and record satisfaction rating.

final_message.description: Optional final message to send before closing

send_final_message.description: Whether to send the final_message to the customer

closed_by.description: User ID of the agent closing the conversation

tools.name: delete_conversation
tools.description: Soft-delete a conversation and its messages. The data is retained for audit purposes but no longer visible in listings.

tools.name: assign_conversation
tools.description: Assign or reassign a conversation to a support agent. Supports notification of both new and previous assignees, priority changes, and assignment notes.

assigned_to.description: Agent/user ID to assign to, or null to unassign

assignment_reason.description: Reason for the assignment or reassignment

notify_previous_assignee.description: Notify the previous assignee about reassignment

tools.name: escalate_conversation
tools.description: Escalate a conversation to a higher priority level or different team. Use when a conversation needs elevated attention or specialist handling.

escalation_notes.description: Additional context for the escalation target

tools.name: create_sender
tools.description: Register a new authorized sender email address for a project. Senders must be verified before they can be used to send emails. After creating, the sender will need domain verification.

name.description: Display name for the sender (e.g., 'Support Team')

description.description: Optional description or notes about this sender

tools.name: list_senders
tools.description: List authorized sender email addresses for the project. Filter by email, active status, or search by name/description.

search.description: Search in email, name, and description (optional)

tools.name: get_sender
tools.description: Get details of a specific authorized sender by ID, including email, display name, active status, and verification info.

tools.name: update_sender
tools.description: Update an existing authorized sender - change email, display name, description, or active status.

tools.name: delete_sender
tools.description: Delete an authorized sender email address. Cannot delete senders that are actively used by running campaigns.

events.name: messaging.inbound_message_processed
events.description: Inbound message received from customer (email reply, SMS, etc.)

events.name: messaging.message_bounced
events.description: Message delivery failed (hard or soft bounce)

events.name: messaging.conversation_updated
events.description: Conversation status, priority, or assignment changed

events.name: messaging.campaign_completed
events.description: Campaign finished sending to all recipients
